name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I am writing to ask you to make a $3 contribution to help our campaign fight back against this attack. I cannot do this alone. Please chip in today.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9)
Sisters and Brothers -
Earlier today, we learned that an organization called “One Nation” launched a multi-million dollar ad campaign attacking Medicare for All on the eve of our first debate.
Now make no mistake, this is not just an attack on Medicare for All, it is an attack on our campaign — which is leading the fight to guarantee health care as a right in this country.
No great surprise!
The insurance industry and their allies will spend millions of dollars in their attack ads to defeat us. Their goal is to continue making billions in profits off a dysfunctional system that leaves millions of Americans struggling to get the care and prescription drugs they need.
They see us as a threat to their greed, and they are right. We are a threat. But we have to fight back. We cannot let these attacks go unanswered. So I have to ask for your help:
[**Can you make a $3 contribution to our campaign today? Help us fight back against the multi-million dollar attack ad against Medicare for All and against our campaign.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/12469?refcode=em190620-2-60&t=26&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9)
The goal of a sane health care system should be to keep people well, not to give CEOs outrageous compensation packages and make stockholders rich while leaving tens of millions uninsured.
That ends when we are in the White House.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/12469?refcode=em190620-2-60&t=27&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=28&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=29&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=30&refcode2=1777_150750_UyDiY9&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=31&akid=1777%2E150750%2EUyDiY9).

name: Patricia Lassiter
subject: “I told them they could kiss my ⬛️⬛️⬛️ if they thought I would stand down for this.”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pride-month?refcode=em062119&noskip=true&recurring=true&amount=3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pride-month?refcode=em062119&noskip=true&recurring=true&amount=3]
Dear Friend –
With most politicians, it can be difficult to know where they really stand on important issues. But not Teresa. She’s been a fighter for progressive causes for decades, and that’s why I’m proud to support her in this campaign and excited to show you another example of her leadership.
Teresa made headlines this week after a fiery speech at Georgia Equality’s Evening for Equality . She talked about being in the trenches of the fight for equality with everyone in our LGBTQ+ family and why that fight continues today.
The best evidence of what someone is going to do is what they’ve already done. So, I’d like to tell you about Teresa’s history. If you don’t identify as LGBTQ+, it can be easy to forget that not that long ago LGBTQ+ rights were virtually non-existent: no right to marry who you love, no right to adopt a child with who you love, no right to be with the person you love in their – or your – final hours. For many Georgians, acceptance of the LGBTQ+ community has been a journey – and we are glad that there has been such a positive sea change of acceptance. But Teresa’s been with us from the very beginning – through many battles.
But my favorite story? In 2004, Georgia Republicans, in an effort to divide and conquer to win an election, proposed a statewide referendum to deny same-sex couples the right to marry. It was popular at the time, but that didn’t stop Teresa from writing a letter to the editor in strong opposition. She presciently wrote: “History will record this bigotry toward gay Americans precisely as it has recorded the persecution of blacks, women, and Jews."
To this day, Teresa still says, “I didn’t care if I was ever invited to another dinner party, or placed on another Christmas Card list, or elected to anything – they could kiss my ass if they thought I would stand down for this.”
And while that referendum passed, Teresa kept fighting. She never quit until what was wrong was made right.
I love that about her. And when she’s in the Senate fighting for healthcare or childcare or college loan reform or any of the other pressing issues of today, you’ll love that fighting spirit, too.
Please join me in making a monthly contribution to her campaign. Even $3 a month can help put the fighter Georgia needs in the U.S. Senate. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pride-month?refcode=em062119&noskip=true&recurring=true&amount=3]
Love is love,
Patricia Lassiter

clean_body: [] Dear Friend – With most politicians, it can be difficult to know where they really stand on important issues. But not Teresa. She’s been a fighter for progressive causes for decades, and that’s why I’m proud to support her in this campaign and excited to show you another example of her leadership. Teresa made headlines this week after a fiery speech at Georgia Equality’s Evening for Equality . She talked about being in the trenches of the fight for equality with everyone in our LGBTQ+ family and why that fight continues today. The best evidence of what someone is going to do is what they’ve already done. So, I’d like to tell you about Teresa’s history. If you don’t identify as LGBTQ+, it can be easy to forget that not that long ago LGBTQ+ rights were virtually non-existent: no right to marry who you love, no right to adopt a child with who you love, no right to be with the person you love in their – or your – final hours. For many Georgians, acceptance of the LGBTQ+ community has been a journey – and we are glad that there has been such a positive sea change of acceptance. But Teresa’s been with us from the very beginning – through many battles. But my favorite story? In 2004, Georgia Republicans, in an effort to divide and conquer to win an election, proposed a statewide referendum to deny same-sex couples the right to marry. It was popular at the time, but that didn’t stop Teresa from writing a letter to the editor in strong opposition. She presciently wrote: “History will record this bigotry toward gay Americans precisely as it has recorded the persecution of blacks, women, and Jews." To this day, Teresa still says, “I didn’t care if I was ever invited to another dinner party, or placed on another Christmas Card list, or elected to anything – they could kiss my ass if they thought I would stand down for this.” And while that referendum passed, Teresa kept fighting. She never quit until what was wrong was made right. I love that about her. And when she’s in the Senate fighting for healthcare or childcare or college loan reform or any of the other pressing issues of today, you’ll love that fighting spirit, too. Please join me in making a monthly contribution to her campaign. Even $3 a month can help put the fighter Georgia needs in the U.S. Senate. name: Tim Kaine
subject: Help Doug Jones fight back against Roy Moore
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Roy Moore announced that he would be seeking the Republican nomination again. We can't let him anywhere near the U.S. Senate.
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek:

2017 may feel like a lifetime ago, but it really hasn't been such a long time since we made history in the Alabama special election, defeating Roy Moore and electing Doug Jones. [**Next year, Doug is up for re-election, and we have the chance to make history again by making sure he stays in the Senate.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1773/3e0906/7b71f755/3e3082ed/3259018783/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-doug-jones?source=20190621_1_FR_TK_Jones&amount=30&refcode=20190621_1_FR_TK_Jones&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Next+year+Doug+is+up+for+reelection+and+&utm_campaign=20190621_1_FR_TK_Jones")

Doug is already looking at a tough battle ahead. Just yesterday, his former opponent Roy Moore announced that he would be seeking the Republican nomination again. During his last run, we learned of several credible allegations that Moore sexually abused underage girls. He's also said that America was "great" during the time of slavery. Despite these absolutely disqualifying facts, Moore garnered support for his Senate bid from plenty of Republicans, including President Trump himself. Now he's back, and we can't let him get anywhere near the U.S. Senate.

[**It 's on us to step up and help Doug fight back against Roy Moore and national Republican attacks. Please join me and help Doug Jones win by chipping in today.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1773/3e0906/7b71f755/3e3082ec/3259018783/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-doug-jones?source=20190621_1_FR_TK_Jones&amount=30&refcode=20190621_1_FR_TK_Jones&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+Itrsquos+on+us+to+step+up+and+help+Doug+&utm_campaign=20190621_1_FR_TK_Jones")

The good news is that Doug's got a great chance of winning again next November. He's a strong voice in Washington for preserving access to affordable health care, working for equality for all our communities, and fighting for fair-paying jobs for every American. That's exactly the kind of person I want by my side in the Senate, and I believe that the voters of Alabama agree.

We've seen what we can do when we all come together and put in the work to protect our shared values. We saw it in 2017 in Virginia and Alabama, and in 2018 all across the country! With your help, we can make sure that Doug Jones keeps working hard for every Alabamian, and that someone like Roy Moore stays far away from the U.S. Senate.

Thanks for your help.

_-Tim_

clean_body: Roy Moore announced that he would be seeking the Republican nomination again. We can't let him anywhere near the U.S. Senate. | | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek: 2017 may feel like a lifetime ago, but it really hasn't been such a long time since we made history in the Alabama special election, defeating Roy Moore and electing Doug Jones. "" Doug is already looking at a tough battle ahead. Just yesterday, his former opponent Roy Moore announced that he would be seeking the Republican nomination again. During his last run, we learned of several credible allegations that Moore sexually abused underage girls. He's also said that America was "great" during the time of slavery. Despite these absolutely disqualifying facts, Moore garnered support for his Senate bid from plenty of Republicans, including President Trump himself. Now he's back, and we can't let him get anywhere near the U.S. Senate. "" The good news is that Doug's got a great chance of winning again next November. He's a strong voice in Washington for preserving access to affordable health care, working for equality for all our communities, and fighting for fair-paying jobs for every American. That's exactly the kind of person I want by my side in the Senate, and I believe that the voters of Alabama agree. We've seen what we can do when we all come together and put in the work to protect our shared values. We saw it in 2017 in Virginia and Alabama, and in 2018 all across the country! With your help, we can make sure that Doug Jones keeps working hard for every Alabamian, and that someone like Roy Moore stays far away from the U.S. Senate. Thanks for your help. -Tim | **** ""** | "" "" ""

name: Jason Yates
subject: Faith takeaway points from Trump’s 2020 announcement
body:
| | Critical statements from President Trump’s address
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ieA9bJjT-HRR-jhhZwY0QyI&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190621com01&n=1)
| Peter, On Tuesday evening, President Trump delivered his official 2020 presidential announcement speech to a packed arena of supporters in Florida, where he discussed his accomplishments from his time in office and his goals for the future. _“Tonight, I stand before you to officially launch my campaign for a second term as president of the United States.”_ – President Trump And on the other side of the aisle, Democrats have more than 20 prominent candidates in the race for president. The upcoming debates and months on the campaign trail will soon begin to narrow down the field. We will follow both President Trump’s campaign, the Democratic candidates, and exciting races across the country so that you can keep up with what candidates are saying on the issues related to our Christian faith. Here are three critical statements from President Trump’s address worth noting. 1\. The Sanctity of Life President Trump stated, “Virtually, every top Democrat also now supports taxpayer-funded abortion right up to the moment of birth. Ripping baby straight from the mother's womb, leading Democrats have even opposed measures to prevent the execution of children after birth.” He continued, “You saw that. In Virginia, Republicans believe that every life is a sacred gift from God. That is why I have asked Congress to prohibit extreme late-term abortion.” Top of mind for many Christians in the upcoming presidential election is the topic of life and how we eliminate abortion in this country. President Trump’s comments, and his actions as President, put him at the opposite end of this debate from the top Democratic candidates who support abortion in nearly all cases. There is no greater attack on God’s creation than when we terminate the innocent life of someone made in the very image of God (Genesis 1:27). There is no principle more fundamental than the right to live from conception until natural death. 2\. Standing with Israel “We have repaired America's friendship with our cherished ally, the state of Israel.” Trump continued, “we recognized the true capital of Israel and opened the American embassy in Jerusalem and we recognized Israeli sovereignty over the Golan Heights.” President Trump has reaffirmed his administration’s alliance with Israel, and his strong support has been welcomed by many Christians and Jews alike. In Genesis 12:3 God says of Israel, “I will bless those who bless you, and whoever curses you I will curse.” Israel has long been America’s closest ally in the Middle East, but far more importantly, they have always been God’s chosen people. 3\. Our National Motto And in closing, President Trump said, “As one America, we believe in the American Constitution and the rule of law. We believe in the dignity of work and the sanctity of life. We believe that faith and family, not government and bureaucracy, are the true American way. We believe that children should be taught to love our country, honor our history, and always respect our great American flag, and we will live by the words of our national motto: In God we Trust.” Scripture says, “Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord” (Psalm 33:12). As Christians, we must always look to elect leaders who defend our values and believe that God is the ultimate authority in this nation. We will be hearing a lot more from the campaign trail in the coming months, and will continue to bring you faith updates as we pray for God’s will to be done in our nation, think about the issues from a biblical perspective, and remember to do our part and vote. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190621com01&n=2)
* * *
[Support My Faith Votes with your tax deductible donation.](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66idsHszZ_cNjdrkrk6Ct22R5LqpO-pYuvVIOOj4RsxM_Q&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190621com01&n=3) _My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement that motivates, equips and activates Christians in America to vote in every election, transforming our communities and influencing our nation with biblical truth. By partnering with national faith leaders, My Faith Votes provides resources to help Christians Pray, Think, and Act to create an America where God is honored in the public square._

body:
**

Good morning Peter,
------------------------------------------------------------

$10,000 IN 10 DAYS

June 30th is the end of the quarter. With your support, we will not only clear this monumental hurdle - we will do so with flying colors! We are doing well right now, and we hope to reach our goal of raising $10,000 over the next 10 days. To achieve this, we are going to need your help. Donate today (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=47ccd14821-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_06_20_09_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-47ccd14821-32267927) !

Because of this important deadline, donors will receive:

-For a donation of $1 or more: A bumper sticker for your car!
-For $5 or more: A bumper sticker and a Marc Flores button!
-For $20 or more: A T-Shirt, a button and a bumper sticker!

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=47ccd14821-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_06_20_09_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-47ccd14821-32267927

Our Presence in the Community

We have been active in the community, and in growing our online presence! Our Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/marcfloresforcongress/?ref=bookmarks&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=47ccd14821-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_06_20_09_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-47ccd14821-32267927) page is constantly growing, and our volunteer base is expanding. See how we are doing this by watching our first video campaign!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=i6PFKuvHHQI&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=47ccd14821-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_06_20_09_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-47ccd14821-32267927
Watch our first video campaign here, and don't forget to like and subscribe!

Thank you for all that you do! Your support moves us forward. Stay tuned for the launch of our online store, updates on policy, and exciting upcoming events!

Best wishes,

Marc Flores

* Republicans keep control. Mitch McConnell remains majority leader. Nothing changes.

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Add your name: It's time to ban private prisons and detention centers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Right now, private prison companies make money from keeping people incarcerated and they spend some of that cash to lobby Washington to continue the cycle of mass incarceration. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e168/45c30650/161c49ef1/669be602/3077902288/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
You know who helped lead the Trump administration's inhumane immigration policies?

John Kelly, the former White House chief of staff.

And you know who just got hired by a company that profits off of those policies?

The very same John Kelly, cashing in on cruelty. He now helps oversee a company that makes money from the largest detention centers for unaccompanied migrant children -- where kids are reportedly kept in unsanitary, prison-like conditions.

It's just one example of how Washington works hand-in-hand with private prison companies, who spend millions on lobbyists, campaign contributions, and revolving-door hires -- all to turn our criminal and immigration policies into ones that prioritize making them rich instead of keeping us safe.

We need significant reform in both criminal justice and in immigration, to end mass incarceration and all of the unnecessary, cruel, and punitive forms of immigration detention that have taken root in the Trump Administration. The first step is to end this private profiteering off cruelty.

**That 's why today, I'm proposing my plan to root out once and for all the profit incentives perverting our criminal and immigration systems.

[Add your name if you agree: We need to ban private prisons and detention centers and make sure companies aren't profiting off of exploiting people who are incarcerated.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e168/45c30650/161c49ef1/669be605/3077902288/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDYyMWVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-ban-private-prisons?source=20190621em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Here's the plan:

**1\. Ban private prisons and detention facilities.**

There should be no place in America for profiting off putting more people behind bars or in detention. That's why I will shut down the use of federal private detention facilities by ending all contracts that the Bureau of Prisons, ICE, and the U.S. Marshals Service have with private detention providers. And I will extend these bans to states and localities by conditioning their receipt of federal public safety funding on their use of public facilities.

**2\. Stop contractors from charging service fees for essential services.**

Companies shouldn't be able to treat incarcerated individuals as captive profit centers. We should prohibit contractors from charging incarcerated and detained people for basic services they need, like phone calls, bank transfers, and healthcare. I'll also keep contractors from imposing exploitative price markups on other services they provide, like commissary or package services. And I'll prohibit companies from charging for re-entry, supervision, and probation services, too -- because no one should have to pay for their own incarceration, whether it's inside a facility or outside of one.

**3\. Hold contractors accountable by expanding oversight, transparency, and enforcement.**

We can keep contractors from cutting corners to make a quick buck by setting enforceable quality standards, regularly auditing and investigating contractors, and terminating their contracts if they fall short. We can prosecute companies that blatantly violate the law. And I'll make sure companies are held accountable no matter who's in the White House by allowing people to bring a lawsuit against abusive contractors who violate their rights.

**Here 's what this all comes down to: The government has a basic responsibility to keep the people in its care safe -- not to use their punishment as an opportunity for profit.

Right now, private prison companies make money from keeping people incarcerated and they spend some of that cash to lobby Washington to continue the cycle of mass incarceration. It's a vicious cycle, but as a grassroots movement, we can have the power to put a stop to it.

[Add your name if you agree. Let's fight to ban private prisons and detention centers, and let's stop profit-driven cruelty against incarcerated people.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e168/45c30650/161c49ef1/669be605/3077902288/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDYyMWVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-ban-private-prisons?source=20190621em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Hundreds of Migrant Caravan Members Found to Have US Criminal Histories: DHS Files
body:
"Hundreds of illegal immigrants attempting to cross the southern border as part of massive migrant caravans were found to have criminal histories..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3d9714e65b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Hundreds of Migrant Caravan Members Found to Have US Criminal Histories: DHS Files**

“Hundreds of illegal immigrants attempting to cross the southern border as part of massive migrant caravans were found to have criminal histories in the U.S., according to newly obtained Department of Homeland Security documents,” Brooke Singman reports for Fox News.

“The files detailed one migrant caravan of nearly 8,000 individuals that started toward the border in October 2018 and arrived south of California by December. According to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), 660 of them had U.S. criminal convictions—with 40 convicted of assault or aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, and three convicted of murder.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fd6ef35ff1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “For months, America’s farmers, ranchers, and manufacturers have been waiting on Nancy Pelosi and the Democrat-led House to do their job and consider trade deals that put America First, like the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA). The agreement as it currently stands would bring our trade relationship with our neighbors into the 21st century while creating another 176,000 new jobs and more than $68 billion in new investment in the U.S. economy,” Rep. Jeff Duncan (R-SC) writes in [Greenville Business Magazine](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=94ed565bbc&e=3ee4985eea). Democrats concede that “NAFTA was not a good deal for American workers and didn’t result in bigger paychecks,” Rep. Duncan says. It’s time to fix that.
---
|
---
| “With almost 500,000 new manufacturing jobs created in the United States since he took office, President Trump can proudly stand on his policies which are beginning the revitalization of America with some of the latest high-tech, advanced manufacturing factories in the world,” Richard Manning writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1b6840b315&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| Carla Provost, chief of the U.S. Border Patrol, testified before Congress yesterday that her officers have been working in overdrive just to keep up with the record pace of illegal immigration, Timothy Meads writes in [_Townhall_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=15d626b992&e=3ee4985eea). “If we do not [have] some kind of consequence for violating the law and illegally crossing our borders, then I don't know what I am here for, in all honesty,” she said.
---
|
---
| “If Congress truly wants to address the humanitarian crisis on our southern border, there is no better solution than ensuring the Office of Refugee Resettlement is adequately funded to handle this crisis,” Sen. Mike Braun (R-IN) writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d7d500db4d&e=3ee4985eea). “This should not be a partisan issue, but for those Democrats who claim to be concerned with the living conditions of children on our southern border, they will have no one to blame but themselves if they block this legislation that is even supported by the New York Times' editorial board.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9a145f2011&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=39ac686087&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=f07b7115bf)

It’s time to fix that. | | “With almost 500,000 new manufacturing jobs created in the United States since he took office, President Trump can proudly stand on his policies which are beginning the revitalization of America with some of the latest high-tech, advanced manufacturing factories in the world,” Richard Manning writes in . | | Carla Provost, chief of the U.S. Border Patrol, testified before Congress yesterday that her officers have been working in overdrive just to keep up with the record pace of illegal immigration, Timothy Meads writes in . “If we do not [have] some kind of consequence for violating the law and illegally crossing our borders, then I don't know what I am here for, in all honesty,” she said. | | “If Congress truly wants to address the humanitarian crisis on our southern border, there is no better solution than ensuring the Office of Refugee Resettlement is adequately funded to handle this crisis,” Sen. Mike Braun (R-IN) writes in the . “This should not be a partisan issue, but for those Democrats who claim to be concerned with the living conditions of children on our southern border, they will have no one to blame but themselves if they block this legislation that is even supported by the New York Times' editorial board.” | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

Our welfare system has too many hand-outs when it should be giving a hand-up | | |
# Handouts keep Americans stuck
---
| | Hard times happen to all of us, and sometimes folks need a little extra help to get back on their feet.

Our current welfare system has robbed many individuals of the dignity that comes with hard work. Requiring welfare recipients to take a drug test and get a job gives people the opportunity to better themselves.

For many able-bodied adults our welfare system encourages a life of dependence on government, rather than serving as a hand-up to a life of independence.
---

| I have fought and won on welfare reform in Kansas, and I will be at the forefront of this fight in the US Senate.

You can read about this and my six other non-negotiable commitments to you, in my [Contract with Kansas](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=bcbff2fec3&e=6882b657f8).

Without a fighter in the U.S. Senate, issues like this will continue to be ignored. subject: I'm personally asking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:

| | I'm emailing again about Mitch McConnell's latest comment with a personal request:

**[Will you sign my petition demanding McConnell and the Republican Senate take immediate action to safeguard our elections? If we get 1,000 signatures before midnight I will personally hand deliver them to Mitch McConnell's office. >>](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82608/232dfd1/96957d9/62a10fa2/2360482831/VEsH/ "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-petition-all?source=20190621_em_pet_baer_b1_all")**

It's becoming dangerously clear to me that security of the 2020 election is in doubt.

Donald Trump openly admitted he would accept help from a foreign power to win his re-election, and we're fending off cyber attacks from adversaries every day.

Yet Mitch McConnell is sitting on a bill passed by House Democrats that would protect us from future election meddling.

_(It 's almost as if he's convinced that after the Russians helped Trump win once, he'd like to see them intervene again.)_

[**It 's reckless and cynical. Can you join me in calling on McConnell to take action and protect the 2020 election immediately? >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82608/232dfd1/96957d9/62a10fa2/2360482831/VEsE/ "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-petition-all?source=20190621_em_pet_baer_b1_all")

I worked in the State Department under President Obama. I challenged Putin, the Chinese Communist Party, and other authoritarian regimes head-on. I saw my fair share of corrupt governments and botched elections.

McConnell gets the intel. He knows our election is vulnerable -- from both foreign interference AND Donald Trump himself.

[**We can 't let him get away with this. Will you sign my petition immediately and demand McConnell protect the 2020 election? I need 1,000 signatures before midnight. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82608/232dfd1/96957d9/62a10fa2/2360482831/VEsF/ "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-petition-all?source=20190621_em_pet_baer_b1_all") Thanks, Dan

| | **Housing is a human right. ** But for too many Americans, affordable, safe, and healthy housing is out of reach--**forcing them to pay 40, 50, or even 60 percent of their income in rent, and forcing others on the streets.** As the former Secretary of HUD under President Obama, I've seen the challenges that Americans face in housing--from rising rents, to gentrification, to housing discrimination, to homelessness. We need a president who will match the urgency of this issue with concrete, bold plans. [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd86/3eb3e2fc/42ba451f/74491d7c/2144754182/VEsE/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-housing-part-1/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout&source=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout") _My visit to Midwest Country Estates, a mobile home park in Waukee, Iowa_ That's why my new [People First Housing platform](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd86/3eb3e2fc/42ba451f/74491d7b/2144754182/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/-709fcc24/3eb7284b/48407c01/744906af/793787327/VEsF/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=3+-+People+First+Housing+platform&utm_campaign=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout&source=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout") includes three critical components to ensure every American can access safe, affordable housing for them and their families:
1. [Solving the Rental Affordability Crisis and Ending Homelessness](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd86/3eb3e2fc/42ba451f/74491d7a/2144754182/VEsC/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-housing-part-1/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=4+-+Solving+the+Rental+Affordability+Crisis+&utm_campaign=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout&source=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout")
2. [Providing Fair Housing to All Americans and Aligning Housing Policy with Climate Goals](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd86/3eb3e2fc/42ba451f/74491d79/2144754182/VEsD/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-housing-part-2/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=5+-+Providing+Fair+Housing+to+All+Americans+&utm_campaign=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout&source=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout")
3. [Boosting Homeownership and Holding Wall Street Accountable](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd86/3eb3e2fc/42ba451f/74491d78/2144754182/VEsA/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-housing-part-3/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=6+-+Boosting+Homeownership+and+Holding+Wall+&utm_campaign=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout&source=20190621_em_pol_housing-rollout")

Together, we can ensure that every American has access to safe, affordable and healthy housing. As your president, I will put forward policies that accept nothing less. Thank you,

subject: More About Our Food...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
It is time for us to fix our farm and food system. Farmers are struggling. Obesity and diabetes rates are rising. And there are food deserts all over the USA.

We need leaders that will put together a wide-ranging plan that strengthens the USA's farms. Big and small. A plan that gets rid of food deserts and promotes healthy eating.

| Friend, quickly checking in as the days count down to the first presidential debate next week (John's debating on Thursday the 27th).

We're working around the clock to build up our team of volunteers, so we can get John's message out to as many voters as possible before the debate.

subject: Save the Date
body:
Today marks the first day of summer (though you might not know it from the recent weather here in Indiana), and it’s already shaping up to be a...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/save-the-date?e=1acd9bb3d6)
https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]
Save the Date

Today marks the first day of summer (though you might not know it from the recent weather here in Indiana), and it’s already shaping up to be a busy season for Indiana politics.

First, we’ve got our municipal races starting to heat up. Last week, I told you about our big municipal candidate training, “Battleground Indiana: Winning Indiana’s Cities and Towns,” (http://indiana.gop/battlegroundindiana?mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) and I’m excited to tell you it was a big success. We brought together mayoral candidates, city and town council candidates, campaign teams and future candidates – and there’s no doubt that we’ve got some very talented public servants (current and future) in Indiana’s cities and towns. Check out some photos from the big event here (https://www.facebook.com/indgop/videos/2283544625059812/?__xts__%5b0%5d=68.ARB-eS1E1AW-Rz1ZrkwwYElcmrk6bLHM9wSDfP4sKNwscc8oXNWudxO6_RXhdYyyaCtX846Cqi05OdKudttz_5ocVOKV6elHgUIINkIRdH0t6WTdhJArA4Lm4A_W1P6GifmN-oKr2G2jJBlf1uZzzOIYaZDjSwlaYjEa_0kQQvoxdUY7zOqSol27eqeSgzBucqyrbd766xzPTVVehi0mnUJ-e-aycZ58E7Y_rfvE9TUNJvUuco-RTbw7c504HrsWylSFzpx0T7Et2LKq53W0-xPxbA58wP8R10fmKfF7JMbF4RyUnj_DlgPjWUH3_cvhny1bD2ASwNQvg2S03ggwCy1i8n6GDQ&__tn__=-R&mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

And then next month, I’d really encourage you to save a date on your calendar: Saturday, July 13, 2019. More details will be coming soon (stay tuned!), but Governor Eric Holcomb is hosting a big event celebrating Indiana and the incredible momentum Hoosiers are achieving together, and it’s going to be an event you won’t want to miss. You can RSVP for the event here (https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

So save the date – remember: Saturday, July 13, 2019 – and keep an eye on your local municipal candidates this year. There’s no doubt that they’ve got big plans in the works for your community.

Click here to view (and then SHARE) on Facebook the RSVP link for Governor Holcomb's big event! (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/posts/1494207367380316?__xts__[0]=68.ARC9js-KoJF25oXZ78rLpRmiNMSnvCPfS2zLxDxaLcp0kSSenHVvM2lKEn-d7J4ijegOKPtFH9SgbQ4IL2rKsRobvFI1ridHrGXp8Vj8IH3L0aE8XkyfjdCH4WTKjzNoYQPCXWLraLNpAR_GSv1RkD7BiuQoNWjO4tiQNvJzc3nFsbttuYXQpGz49zuHOnomjtN-lYdM79DqjVR8v1YVUIT05vFR0dlSJFjARrXNGhBf9IvgUvm-dyeBlDwr4dGQi9zNf-tgb_wg_Jx26f0FPXNUKZLiOhlEragXtteJ2GkN0mq7cSAkFq_XTsi_uWz7JZCn8tXDy1vtNEsTVWGiaWkxYg&__tn__=-R&mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

-Kyle Hupfer
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was

A Week for Jobs (& Baseball)

This week, Governor Eric Holcomb was focused on Hoosier jobs – attracting them, celebrating them, and helping Hoosiers build the skills to earn them. This included:
* Cutting the ribbon on Blue Buffalo’s new pet food manufacturing facility (https://www.pal-item.com/story/news/local/2019/06/17/skilled-dedicated-employees-boost-blue-buffalos-beginnings/1475237001/?mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) in Richmond in East Central Indiana, which is creating 165 new Hoosier jobs
* Joining Ivanka Trump and Fortune 100 CEOs for a meeting of President Trump’s American Workforce Policy Advisory Board (https://abc11.com/politics/ivanka-trump-visits-charlotte-to-talk-us-manufacturing-/5351583/?mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , which is working to scale-up successful models like we have in Indiana to help people earn high-demand job skills
* Chairing a meeting of the Indiana Economic Development Corporation (https://www.wimsradio.com/2019/06/18/governor-in-chesterton-wednesday-to-chair-a-joint-public-meeting-of-the-board-of-directors-of-the-indiana-economic-development-corporation-iedc-and-the-indiana-economic-development-foundation-inc/?mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) at Urschel Laboratories in Chesterton in Northwest Indiana
* Joining the Northeast Indiana Regional Partnership for their lunch (http://www.journalgazette.net/news/local/20190620/governor-to-name-area-first-talent-region?fbclid=IwAR1WO1gkzKzPERlMhd0OF_qPPsYN2gZL619FzMqOZhvfe-NV3PZtBja0yCE&mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) in Fort Wayne, and helping announce the inaugural 21st Century Talent Region, which will help the region attract and retain top talent

And then, when he was in South Bend, Governor Holcomb threw out the first pitch at the Midwest League All Star Game, hosted at South Bend’s Four Winds Field. Of course, he threw the pitch right over home plate!
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf2JItBHI6v3IIKv9yky6sxbXMkPhrgArt0fgP8QrDOUW1Gfw/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&fbclid=IwAR18KM_ZWmaNtWoQT1Evs6YIr2qEKSRF73swoC9dwa7-jUEQ5RoZpn_RLgU&mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Get President Trump on the Ballot!

This week, President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence officially announced that they’re all-in for 2020 and are running for re-election! Hoosiers are fired-up to re-elect President Trump, but first we’ve got to get him on the ballot in Indiana – and that requires ballot petition signatures in each of Indiana’s congressional districts.

Would you commit to help collect petition signatures among your family, friends and neighbors? It’s a very easy way to stand with President Donald Trump right now, and help him continue to make America great!

Just fill out this form (https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf2JItBHI6v3IIKv9yky6sxbXMkPhrgArt0fgP8QrDOUW1Gfw/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&fbclid=IwAR18KM_ZWmaNtWoQT1Evs6YIr2qEKSRF73swoC9dwa7-jUEQ5RoZpn_RLgU&mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and we’ll be in touch soon to get you started!
http://lugarseries.com/?mc_cid=9a1b8d97c2&mc_eid=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=a96126de2b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Are you a woman interested in expanding your civic engagement? Are you looking for an opportunity to make an impact in your community and build your leadership skills? The Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series, now in its 30th year, was created for you!

name: James Mackler
subject: Pride Month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Tell Mitch McConnell to bring the Equality Act up for a vote.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa24b/18d6a294/4be1a8f6/26d68dc3/4120232487/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | Friends, We've come a long way in the fight for equality for LGBTQ Americans. I am proud to have gotten to know and work alongside so many wonderful LGBTQ friends, family members, and neighbors. I am even more proud to stand with them in calling for equality for all. Last month, the House of Representatives passed the Equality Act, would extend key provisions of the Civil Rights Act to include sexual orientation and gender identity. But right now, Mitch McConnell has refused to put the bill on the Senate floor. [**_Tell Mitch McConnell to bring the Equality Act up for a vote._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa24b/18d6a294/4be1a8f6/26d68dc0/4120232487/VEsE/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190621z") My father-in-law shares a loving relationship with his husband, whom he was able to marry after 23 years of being together. It pains me to think that the two of them can still be discriminated against. I want my two daughters to grow up in a world where no one can legally discriminate against their grandfathers. I want my daughters to be proud of who they are, and respectful of all people regardless of their sexual orientation or gender. | [](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa24b/18d6a294/4be1a8f6/26d68dc0/4120232487/VEsF/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190621z")
---
No one should have to live in fear of being fired or discriminated against because of who they are or who they love. It shouldn't matter which state they live in, members of the LGBTQ community need equal rights and equal protections under the law. As it stands now, that isn't the case.
This Pride Month, while we celebrate the progress our country has made, we must also remember the sacrifices so many made to get us to this point. And we should honor them by advancing non-discrimination protections for LGBTQ individuals nationwide.
[**_This is long overdue. Call on Mitch McConnell to pass the Equality Act once and for all._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa24b/18d6a294/4be1a8f6/26d68dc0/4120232487/VEsC/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190621z")
Thank you,
James

|

name: Faiz Shakir
subject: I am asking you directly
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
We can't come up short.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1782%2E150750%2ENGqaiL)
Really quick, before the day gets away.
Here's the truth: Joe Biden is traveling around the country raising _a lot_ of money for his campaign at fundraising events with billionaires, corporate lobbyists, and Wall Street executives. And we can't afford to fall too far behind.
Right now we're approaching an important FEC fundraising deadline – the first full FEC filing period since Bernie got in the race. And we're aiming to generate as many individual donations as possible before it comes to a close.
